meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' trap with a high-velocity SaaS starter kit. Learn how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your MVP in record time.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Use a high-velocity SaaS starter kit to scaffold a functional MVP in hours. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural blueprints and Claude Code for iterative development, you transform abstract concepts into monetizable software, effectively bypassing the 'no idea' paralysis through rapid, market-validated execution.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You have the drive to be a founder, but you are paralyzed by the 'no project ideas' syndrome—a cognitive trap that keeps your bank account stagnant while you wait for a lightning bolt of inspiration that may never strike.

Real founders don't wait for inspiration; they engineer it. By shifting your focus from 'finding the idea' to 'shipping the infrastructure,' you create a sandbox where ideas are born from iteration. This guide treats your development process as a lean manufacturing line, using modern AI tooling to strip away the friction of boilerplate and configuration.

steps.title: Define the Value Proposition
steps.description: Before writing code, use AI to map out a micro-SaaS niche. Focus on B2B pain points that are currently underserved by bloated legacy software.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Generate 5 high-potential, low-complexity micro-SaaS ideas in the productivity space, including a target persona and a clear monetization model for each.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project spec for a micro-SaaS that automates email scheduling for small law firms.'

steps.title: Scaffold with a Starter Kit
steps.description: Initialize your project using a battle-tested stack. A robust starter kit handles Auth, DB, and Stripe integration out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a boilerplate project structure using Next.js, Supabase, and Tailwind CSS. Ensure the directory structure follows industry best practices for scalability.

steps.title: Configure Database Schema
steps.description: Define your data models early. Your schema is the backbone of your SaaS business logic and user management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a Supabase-compatible database schema for a subscription-based SaaS. Include tables for users, profiles, subscriptions, and usage logs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Create the SQL migration files for users and subscription tables in the /supabase folder.'

steps.title: Implement Core Business Logic
steps.description: Focus on the 'Single Feature' that provides the most value. Strip away everything else to achieve speed-to-market.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core utility function that transforms user input into the primary service output. Ensure robust error handling and type safety.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement a service layer function that processes user requests and updates the database record.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Don't wait for perfection. Deploy to Vercel or a similar platform to get your MVP in front of real users immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for production readiness, including environment variables, security headers, and domain configuration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review the current codebase and provide a deployment readiness report.'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Time to First Dollar' over feature parity; if your app doesn't have a checkout button, it's just a hobby.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests as you go; it forces the model to understand the logic flow, reducing bugs by 40%.
Never build custom auth; integrate Clerk or Supabase Auth to save 20+ hours of security hardening.
Build a 'kill-switch' into your MVP that allows you to turn off features easily if users aren't engaging with them.
Adopt a 'Ship-on-Friday' mentality to force yourself to resolve technical debt before the weekend.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, implement a basic feedback loop using a tool like Crisp or Hotjar to gather user insights, then use Claude Code to iterate based on actual usage data.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for the void and start building for the market. By mastering these tools, you turn the 'no project ideas' problem into a competitive advantage. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
